rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2025, line 9, DEFENDANT NICOLAS COLON'S Motion To Vacate Bank Levy Due To Improper Service Judgment debtor Nicolas Colon's unopposed motion to vacate bank levy due to improper service is granted. The levy issued on February 14, 2025 against Colon's Bank of America account is hereby vacated. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL VS. SANTOS & URRUTIA ASSOCIATES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Rodrigo Santos And Impose Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFFS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Rodrigo Santos And Impose Sanctions The City and County of San Francisco's unopposed request to compel the deposition of defendant Rodrigo Santos is granted. Some scheduling issues can be expected in light of the fact that Santos is in custody but the City has shown good cause for the deposition and it ultimately should occur. The City shall meet and confer with Santos's counsel within five court days after entry of this order to find a mutually agreeable date. The deposition shall occur by April 17, 2025, unless the parties otherwise agree. If meet and confer efforts do not result in a mutually agreeable date, or if Santos's counsel does not participate in meet and confer efforts, the City may, after five court days have passed, unilaterally set the deposition on any day convenient to the City. The City seeks sanctions. CCP sec. 2025.450(g)(1) requires the Court to impose sanctions unless it finds the deponent acted with substantial justification or there are circumstances that render imposition of sanctions unjust. The court does not find that Santos acted with substantial justification but reduces the requested sanctions amount to reflect the difficulties of setting a deposition date under these circumstances. The court awards $4750 in sanctions payable by Santos to the City within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH WILLIAMS VS. SERVE ALL, HELP ALL, INC., DBA TEH NONPROFIT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Re: Plaintiff To Submit To An Independent Metal/Psychiatric Examination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2025, line 5, DEFENDANT SERVE ALL, HELP ALL, INC., DBA THE NONPROFIT ALLIANCE OF CONSUMER ADVOCATES, A BUSINESS ENTITY'S Motion To Compel Discovery Re: Plaintiff To Submit To An Independent Metal/Psychiatric Examination The unopposed motion of defendant Serve All, Help All, Inc. to compel plaintiff Kenneth Williams to submit to a mental examination is granted. The court finds good cause pursuant to CCP 2032.320(a). Williams is ordered to submit to an examination by Alexandra Clarfield PhD via Telehealth or other approved video provider on April 22, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. unless the parties mutually agree otherwise. Williams shall complete the intake form to be provided one week in advance of the examination. The examination shall last no longer than three hours and Clarfield may administer up to three brief standardized psychological tests based on her findings in the interview. The court incorporates the conditions of interview and scope of interview set out in numbered paragraphs 2 and 3 of page 5 of Defendant's memorandum in support of its motion to compel IME. The court does not anticipate ordering further testing; all defendants are admonished to meet and confer with moving defendant Serve All, Help All, Inc. to have input into the tests that will be conducted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: DR. DOE VS. KAISER PERMANENTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination [Ccp 2032.310(A)]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2025, line 6, DEFENDANTS KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination [Ccp 2032.310(A)] The unopposed motion of defendants Kaiser Foundation Hospitals and the Permanente Medical Group, Inc. is granted. The court finds good cause to order the mental examination. (CCP 2032.320(a).) The court will endorse the proposed order submitted by Defendants, except that paragraph 6 of the proposed order submitted by Defendants, relating to remedies if Plaintiff does not comply, will be stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure contains remedies a party may seek if the party to be examined fails to appear for and complete an ordered examination. rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. NHAN H TRUONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, March 26, 2025, line 8, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission is granted. Defendant Nhan H. Truong failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, and the genuineness of any documents attached, is deemed admitted by Defendant. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Plaintiff does not seek sanctions. But sanctions are mandatory in this circumstance. (CCP 2033.280(c) ["It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion."].) The court awards sanctions of $100 payable by Defendant to Plaintiff within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
